mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.0% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% and increases by more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 6.1% is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and HOUST falls below 950 thousand units simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 965.0 is near trigger level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 110.5 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power that supports corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate margin expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 110.3034 is near expansion threshold
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 52% of high INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 137.5 million persons combined with low unemployment (UNRATE below 6.2%) signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 137.5 million AND UNRATE < 6.2% simultaneously for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137473.0 and UNRATE at 6.1% both near trigger conditions
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal has lower predictive power than UNRATE alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 220 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening regime that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 219.181 and FEDFUNDS at 2.0% are at trigger boundaries
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not significantly higher than individual components (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 11750 billion dollars signals strong economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 > 11750 billion chained 2017 dollars in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11740.3 is below expansion threshold
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or SPX produces negative returns in more than 55% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below 110.0 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE above 6.0%) signals supply-side weakness and demand destruction that compresses both margins and growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 110.0 AND UNRATE rises above 6.0% within the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.3034 and UNRATE at 6.1% show mixed signals near thresholds
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal explains less variance than UNRATE alone in walk-forward tests (R² difference < 0.01).
